skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: An object's average acceleration, in meters per second squared, is the change in the object's speed, in meters per second, divided by the time during which the change occurred, in seconds. A certain object's speed changes by 45 meters per second in 6 seconds. What is this object's average acceleration, in meters per second squared, during this time period?

skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$x + 26 = y$
$(x + 26)^2 = y$
A solution to the given system of equations is $(x, y)$. What is a possible value of $x$?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: Lao walks at a speed of 2 miles per hour and runs at a speed of 6 miles per hour. He walks for w hours and runs for r hours for a combined total of 10 miles. Which equation represents this situation?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$f(x) = 50x + 12$
The given function $f$ estimates the distance a train has traveled, in kilometers, from a station in a certain city $x$ hours after crossing the city border. What is the best interpretation of 12 in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Between the station and the city border, the train traveled an estimated total distance of 12 kilometers.

choices.label: B
choices.text: After crossing the city border the train traveled at an estimated speed of 12 kilometers per hour.

choices.label: C
choices.text: After crossing the city border the train traveled an estimated total distance of 12 kilometers.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Between the station and the city border, the train traveled at an estimated speed of 12 kilometers per hour.

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$\frac{1}{3}(x+7)-\frac{1}{2}(x+7)=-8$
What value of x is the solution to the given equation?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function $g$ is defined by $g(x)=\frac{4}{9}x-24$. What is the $x$-coordinate of the $x$-intercept of the graph of $y=g(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: Two students are playing a game. In the first round, Player 1 answers 49 questions. If an answer is correct, Player 1 earns 1 point. If an answer is incorrect, Player 2 will earn 1 point instead. The graph shows $y=f(x)$, where $y$ is the number of points Player 2 will earn when $x$ is the number of points Player 1 earns. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the point (49, 0) in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 49 points, Player 2 will earn 49 points.

choices.label: B
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 49 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.

choices.label: C
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 49 points.

choices.label: D
choices.text: When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: Which expression is a factor of $3x^2 - 5x + 6x - 10$?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The graph shows the momentum $y$, in newton-seconds, of an object $x$ seconds after the object started moving, for $0 \leq x \leq 8$. What is the average rate of change, in newton-seconds per second, in the momentum of the object from $x=2$ to $x=6$?

skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$(x+64)^{2}=-100$
How many distinct real solutions does the given equation have?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage:
$| x-8 | = 11$
What is the sum of the solutions to the given equation?

skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The manager of a gym selected a sample of 159 members at random to estimate the percentage of the gym's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased. From the survey, the manager estimates that 88% of the gym's members would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased, with an associated margin of error of 5.05%. If the survey is repeated with a random sample of 318 members and the results are calculated in the same way, which of the following will be the most likely effect of using the larger random sample compared to the smaller random sample?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The margin of error will be lower.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The margin of error will be higher.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The estimate of the percentage of the gym's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased will be lower.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The estimate of the percentage of the gym's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased will be higher.

skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage:
The maximum temperature on April 1 in degrees Fahrenheit (°F), was recorded each year at a certain weather station for 11 years. The histogram summarizes the recorded data set.
The temperature of 76.2 °F is removed from this data set to create a new data set of 10 temperatures. Which of the following statements must be true?
I. The mean of the new data set is less than the mean of the original data set.
II. The median of the new data set is less than the median of the original data set.

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: For the positive quantities $ h $, $ j $, and $ k $, 44% of $ h $ is equivalent to 62% of $ j $, and $ j $ is equivalent to 22% of $ k $. What percentage of $ k $ is $ h $? (Disregard the % sign when entering your answer. For example, if your answer is 39%, enter 39)

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The graph shows the relationship between the number of shares of stock from Company A, x, and the number of shares of stock from Company B, y, that Sayen can purchase. Which equation could represent this relationship?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: For the exponential function $f$, the value of $f(1)$ is $k$, where $k$ is a constant. Which of the following equivalent forms of the function $f$ shows the value of $k$ as the coefficient or the base?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: In triangle $PQR$, the measure of angle $P$ is $(4x+9)^\circ$, the measure of angle $Q$ is $(5x+4)^\circ,$ and the measure of angle $R$ is $(5y+4)^\circ.$ If side $QR$ is extended through point $R$ to point $S$, and the measure of angle $PRS$ is $(x+y)^\circ$, what is the value of $x + y$?

skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the length of the third side. If a triangle has side lengths of 5 and 9, which inequality represents the possible lengths, x, of the third side of the triangle?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: A right circular cone has a volume of $34,496\pi$ cubic centimeters and the area of its base is $3,136\pi$ square centimeters. What is the slant height, in centimeters, of this cone?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: A quadratic function models the height, in feet, of an object above the ground in terms of the time, in seconds, after the object was launched. According to the model, the object was launched from a height of 0 feet and reached its maximum height of 400 feet 5 seconds after it was launched. Based on the model, what was the height, in feet, of the object 9 seconds after it was launched?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The cost of renting a power washer is \$56 for the first day and \$28 for each additional day. Which of the following functions gives the cost $C(d)$, in dollars, of renting the power washer for $d$ days, where $d$ is a positive integer?

test_name: June 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
passage: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x)=ax^{2}+bx+c$, where $a$, $b$, and $c$ are constants. The graph of $y=f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane passes through the points $(8,0)$ and $(-5,0)$. If $a$ is an integer greater than 1, which of the following could be the value of $a + b$?
